input.thought: Gather authoritative DataForSEO documentation and official pages covering auth, pricing, rate limits, API structure, and MCP availability.

arguments.task: Research DataForSEO using authoritative first-party sources, starting at https://docs.dataforseo.com. Collect evidence for: 1) one-line description of the product/API, 2) authentication methods accepted by the API with exact wording, 3) whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/gated and why, using official pricing/access/signup pages, 4) API surface type REST vs GraphQL and breadth of endpoints/products from docs navigation, 5) rate limit notes from official docs, 6) whether an official or public MCP server exists, prioritizing official docs, GitHub, or MCP registry pages, 7) any notable signals like sandbox/test mode, post-pay model, status/changelog, or API explorer. Prefer docs.dataforseo.com and other official DataForSEO pages. You may also inspect their official GitHub if linked from official pages. Avoid blogs and third-party summaries unless needed for MCP registry confirmation. Return a concise structured summary with exact supporting URLs for each claim.

raw_result.description: DataForSEO provides a REST API platform for SEO, SERP, keywords, backlinks, on-page, and related search data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official auth docs indicate Basic Authentication via the Authorization header using login and password. The browser research notes did not verify any alternative auth methods, so only basic is recorded.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing/help pages indicate self-serve signup with a free unlimited trial and pay-as-you-go usage thereafter; no partner or admin approval gate was evidenced.

api_surface.breadth: Public v3 REST API with multiple product families/endpoints across SEO and search intelligence, including SERP, keywords, backlinks, on-page, and related data APIs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Browser research found an official/public DataForSEO GitHub repository for an MCP server (not independently re-verified in-chat), so MCP existence is marked true but should be human-checked.

raw_result.blocker: Self-serve access appears available, but the evidence gathered suggests production use is pay-as-you-go with a minimum payment after trial; MCP and some access details were not fully re-verified from final page extracts in-chat.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Browser research indicated official rate-limit information is exposed via headers/documentation, but exact request ceilings were not conclusively captured in-chat. Human review recommended for precise limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs indicate REST client libraries for PHP, C#, Java, Python, and TypeScript. Browser research also found signals for Sandbox Best Practices and an API Explorer/Playground help page, plus pay-as-you-go pricing and free trial messaging.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several fields rely on browser-task findings without full final inline extracts: exact pricing/trial constraints, exact rate-limit wording, and independent confirmation of the MCP repository details. These should be verified manually against the cited official pages.
